arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou looks beaten man as Celtic hero roars back at journo's tense grilling
arts.sum: And Sky Sports ‘ reporter Patrick Davison made sure to try and ask the boss for his thoughts on supporter criticism as they chanted “you don’t know what you’re doing” towards their own gaffer. Asked again about losing fans this season, Postecoglou let the journo know how he felt the interview on Sky Sports was going.

arts.ttl: The striker who was stuck with free agency over Celtic move after 2012 Portsmouth administration
arts.sum: “I had offers from Celtic and Rangers, but in the last minutes of the transfer window Pompey’s chief executive didn’t pick up the phone,” Futacs said. Futacs was happy to stay at Portsmouth and snub Celtic despite administration

arts.ttl: Marvin Bartley and Simon Donnelly slam another 'impossible' SPFL suggestion that will enrage Celtic fans
arts.sum: The SPFL could push for a ten-man league – and that has divided opinion since it was announced – but another suggestion was put on the table to the horror of Marvin Bartley and Simon Donnelly . As you can imagine, a hysterical Bartley shut it down and stated it’s ‘impossible’ for this MLS-like style format to be brought in, whilst former Celtic man Donnelly explained that it would make a ‘whole mockery’ of the league system.

arts.ttl: 'It's like I never left' – Jota sends Celtic fans a love letter as winger targets climbing yet another 'mountain'
arts.sum: Jota's won it all before and he's back to do it again in Glasgow's east end

arts.ttl: Matt O'Riley mentor senses Celtic return is ON if mooted mega money exit gets the green light
arts.sum: O'Riley exited Glasgow last summer in a deal which could rise to a Scottish record £30m but he's toiled at Brighton

arts.ttl: Jota reiterates preferred Celtic role, details relationship with Daizen Maeda
arts.sum: Celtic star Jota has once again underlined his preference to play on the left wing but insists he is happy to adapt for the benefit of the team.

arts.ttl: Celtic among Euro scouts sent to watch Belgian pair alongside Brendan Rodgers long-term target
arts.sum: In the wide areas is where has already been scouted with Michel-Ange Balikwisha still a Celtic target to bolster the attacking areas. The Sun report that duo Noah Sadiki and Franjo Ivanovic both fit the profile Celts are looking for but are on the radar of numerous other sides.

arts.ttl: The unseen Celtic difference maker behind Daizen Maeda goal explosion as unsung hero namechecked
arts.sum: Maeda has been red hot and Rodgers thanked one man for his specific role in his firing form

arts.ttl: Five Brendan Rodgers Celtic presser takeaways as boss reveals Adam Idah mindset amid Daizen Maeda striker run
arts.sum: “Like I said, players just turning out, and clubs like ourselves, we are fortunate in relation to, obviously we play more games, so we have a bigger squad and a pool of players that we can pull on, but there are other teams that will, you look at the pre-season this year, Kilmarnock and teams like that in European football, it’s a real challenge.” “But he can do other things, so he’s out on the training field and working on the technical side of his game.”

arts.ttl: Celtic boss praises 'really good' Johnny Kenny, made to wait due to Maeda's form
arts.sum: Celtic forward Johnny Kenny has been catching the eye in training, but the remarkable form of Daizen Maeda continues to keep him and Adam Idah waiting for their chances in Brendan Rodgers' side.
